subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Cards (232 Total)
- **42** vine cards (green)
- **36** wine order cards (purple)
- **38** summer visitor cards (yellow)
- **38** winter visitor cards (blue)
- **18** field cards (large, double-sided)
- **24** Automa cards (large, beige back)
- **36** mama and papa cards (large, pink/blue)

#### Workers & Player Markers
- **30** regular worker meeples (6 colors)
- **6** grande worker meeples
- **1** gray temporary worker
- **6** wake-up tokens (roosters)
- **6** victory point tokens (corks)
- **6** residual-payment tokens (wine bottles)

#### Tokens, Coins, and Structures
- **50** grape and wine glass tokens
- **48** wooden structure tokens (8 unique per color)
- **72** lira coins (52 bronze 1, 12 silver 2, 8 gold 5)
- **1** first-player token (grapes)

#### Boards & References
- **1** game board
- **6** vineyard mats
- **1** rulebook
- **1** quick-reference guide

#### Player-Count Reference List (Step 0 Audit)
- **Supported game size** — 1-6 players.
- **Action-space scaling** — 2 players: left space only; 3-4 players: left + middle; 5-6 players: all three spaces.
- **Wake-up and worker rules** — Same structure for all player counts.
- **Open-information rule** — Same for all player counts.
- **Deck maintenance rule** — Same for all player counts.
- **Advanced setup variant** — Draw 2 mamas + 2 papas, choose 1 of each (optional, not tied to count).

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Center board** — Place the main board in the center.
2. **Deck placement** — Shuffle and place green, yellow, purple, and blue decks in their marked spaces.
3. **Shared supply** — Put coins and shared components near the board.
4. **Player trackers** — Each player places rooster above wake-up chart, cork on VP start, and wine bottle in middle of residual-payment track.
5. **Vineyard mats** — Each player places field cards 5, 6, and 7 field-side up on their mat.
6. **Public info reminder** — Vineyard mats, available workers, lira, and number/type of cards are public information.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **First player randomization** — Randomly choose first player from claimed rooster colors.
2. **Deal setup cards** — Deal each player 1 face-up mama and 1 face-up papa.
3. **Resolve mamas first** — Each mama gives 2 regular workers and opening card draws.
4. **Resolve papas second** — Each papa gives 1 grande worker, usually lira, and a choice (structure vs extra lira, or alternate reward on some papas).
5. **Choice order** — Resolve papa choices clockwise from first player.
6. **Discard setup cards** — Discard mama/papa cards after resources are granted.

#### Optional Variant
- **Advanced setup** — Draw 2 mamas and 2 papas, then choose 1 of each.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **Deck maintenance on** — If any small deck empties, reshuffle its discard to form a new deck immediately.
- **No-discard exception** — If a deck empties and has no discard pile, that deck is unavailable until cards are discarded.
- **Year 1 start** — Begin in Spring after setup resources are complete.
- **Order reminder** — First-player token only affects Spring rooster placement order.
- **Season order source** — Summer/Fall/Winter order follows wake-up chart positions set in Spring.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Spring** — In first-player clockwise order, place roosters on open wake-up rows (1-7) and gain that row's bonus.
2. **Summer** — In wake-up order (top to bottom), place 1 worker on a summer action and resolve, or pass to save workers for winter; continue until all pass.
3. **Fall** — In wake-up order, each player draws either 1 summer visitor or 1 winter visitor card.
4. **Winter** — In wake-up order, place 1 worker on a winter action and resolve; continue until workers are used.
5. **Year End** — Age grapes/wines, return workers, collect residual payments, remove roosters, rotate first-player token counterclockwise.
6. **End check** — If any player is at 20+ VP, finish current year and end game.

#### Wake-Up Row Bonuses
- **Row 1** — No bonus.
- **Row 2** — Draw 1 vine card.
- **Row 3** — Draw 1 wine order card.
- **Row 4** — Gain 1 lira or 1 VP.
- **Row 5** — Draw 1 summer or winter visitor card.
- **Row 6** — Gain 1 VP.
- **Row 7** — Gain the temporary worker for this year.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Worker Placement Core Rules
- **One use per year** — A worker used in summer cannot be used again in winter that year.
- **One worker per turn** — Place exactly 1 worker on your turn.
- **Season lock** — Workers can only go on current season action spaces.
- **Pass lock** — If you pass a season, you cannot place more workers that season.
- **Mandatory action** — If you place a worker, you must perform that action.

#### Player-Count Action-Space Scaling
--- 2 Players ---
- **Available spaces** — Only 1 (left) action space is available for each action.
--- 3-4 Players ---
- **Available spaces** — 2 action spaces (left + middle) are available.
--- 5-6 Players ---
- **Available spaces** — 3 action spaces (left + middle + right) are available.

#### Bonus Space Rules
- **Bonus optional** — You may take the icon bonus if you place on a bonus space.
- **Action mandatory** — You cannot take the bonus without taking the action.

#### Grande Worker Rules
- **Block bypass** — Grande worker may be placed on a fully occupied action.
- **Shared bypass** — Multiple players may use grande this way on the same occupied action.
- **Bonus interaction** — Grande gets a bonus only if placed on the actual bonus action space.

#### Summer Actions (Shown in Provided Excerpt)
- **Gain 1 lira (cart)** — In summer or winter, place a worker on the cart to gain 1 lira; unlimited workers may use this over a year.
- **Play 1 summer visitor** — Play and resolve 1 summer visitor card, then discard it; bonus allows playing 1 additional summer visitor.
- **Draw 1 vine card** — Draw 1 vine card; bonus draws 1 additional vine card.
- **Give a vineyard tour** — Gain 2 lira; bonus gains 1 additional lira.
- **Sell grapes or buy/sell field** — Sell at least 1 grape from crush pad for printed price, OR sell one empty field for its printed lira, OR buy back one sold field at that same price; bonus gains 1 VP.
- **Field restriction** — You cannot sell a field with vines and cannot plant on sold fields.
- **Build 1 structure** — Pay cost and place matching structure token on your mat; only one of each structure type per player; bonus gains 1 lira.
- **Plant 1 vine** — Place a vine card on an empty field or stack on an existing vine in that field; bonus plants 1 additional vine.
- **Field cap** — Total vine value in a field cannot exceed that field's value (5, 6, or 7).

#### Additional Rules in Excerpt
- **Uproot** — Yoke or some visitor cards can return a planted vine card to your hand.
- **VP floor** — Players cannot go below -5 VP.
- **Visitor limit at floor** — At -5 VP, you cannot use a visitor card that requires losing more VP.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Seasonal Tempo Tradeoff
- **Initiative trade** — Early wake-up rows act first but grant weaker bonuses.
- **Compensation trade** — Late wake-up rows act later but grant stronger bonuses.
- **Recurring puzzle** — This timing tradeoff resets every year.

#### Production Pipeline
- **Vine-to-wine chain** — Draw vines, plant legally, harvest grapes, make wine, fill orders.
- **Prerequisite gates** — Some vines require trellis, irrigation, or both before planting.

#### Value vs Price
- **Value meaning** — Grape/wine value is used for wine making and order fulfillment.
- **Price meaning** — Grape price is immediate lira when selling grapes.

#### Pressure & Flexibility
- **Worker scarcity** — Workers are tight because each is single-use per year.
- **Grande release valve** — Grande worker is your main anti-blocking tool.
- **Temporary worker** — Wake-up row 7 grants a regular-use temporary worker for that year only.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
- **End trigger** — If any player reaches 20+ VP, finish the current year, then end the game.
- **No cap** — Players may exceed 20 VP.

#### VP Sources (From Provided Excerpt)
- **Wine orders** — Filling wine orders gives VP (core objective loop).
- **Wake-up bonuses** — Some wake-up rows grant VP.
- **Visitors/effects** — Some visitor cards and board/card effects modify VP, including negative VP costs.

#### Tiebreakers (In Exact Rulebook Order)
1. **Most lira** — Higher lira wins tie.
2. **Wine value** — If still tied, higher total value of wine in cellar wins.
3. **Grape value** — If still tied, higher total value of grapes on crush pad wins.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Build the chain early** — In your first years, prioritize actions that connect vine draw, planting, and future winter conversion/filling.
- **Protect turn order value** — Take early wake-up rows when a contested action slot is critical this year.
- **Take late bonuses with purpose** — Use later wake-up rows only when that bonus fixes a real bottleneck.
- **Save grande for choke points** — Hold grande worker for the one blocked action you cannot miss.
- **Respect field math** — Plant so each field cap (5/6/7) still supports high-value future vines.
- **Keep a pivot card** — Maintain at least one flexible visitor or vine in hand for blocked-board adaptation.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Bonus-first wake-up choices** — Taking late slots for a small bonus while losing key action timing.
- **Season imbalance** — Spending heavily in summer and entering winter without conversion/fill capacity.
- **Illegal or poor planting** — Ignoring trellis/irrigation prerequisites or field limits.
- **Early grande burn** — Spending grande on a replaceable action and getting blocked later on must-have actions.
- **Over-selling grapes** — Taking short-term lira while delaying order fulfillment and VP pace.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Initiative vs bonus** — Decide each spring whether acting first is worth more than wake-up compensation.
- **Cash now vs tempo later** — Decide when grape/field sales fund critical turns versus when they delay scoring momentum.
- **Engine vs tactics** — Choose between persistent vineyard infrastructure and immediate visitor-card swings based on board pressure.

metadata.notes: Written from provided Viticulture Essential Edition excerpt (setup, yearly structure, wake-up chart, worker placement, summer actions, prerequisites, and endgame/tiebreakers).
